name: Bread Pudding
description: Traditional British bread pudding: soaked stale bread baked with suet, dried fruit and mixed spice, finished with a sprinkle of caster sugar. Eat hot or cold.

steps.title: Soak the bread overnight
steps.content: Soak {0001} in cold water in a basin overnight.

steps.title: Drain the bread
steps.content: Squeeze the water out of the bread through a colander.

steps.title: Combine ingredients
steps.content: Put the drained bread in a mixing bowl. Add the dry ingredients: {0002}, {0003}, {0005}, {0006}, {0007} and {0008}. Add {0009}, {0010} and {0004}. Mix thoroughly.

steps.title: Bake
steps.content: Bake in a large, shallow, greased ovenproof dish at 180°C (350°F / Gas Mark 4) for about 1¼–1½ hours. Turn the heat down after 1¼ hours.

steps.title: Finish and serve
steps.content: When cooked and cooled, sprinkle with {0011}. Eat hot or cold.

notes: Original handwritten family recipe — bread soaked overnight is essential. Quantities given in imperial; converted here to metric.
